title: What Generosity Would Feel Right at My Current Level?
subtitle: Journal prompts for building generosity into your wealth as it grows
excerpt: Five journal prompts for thinking about what generosity looks like at your current stage of wealth building. Not the philanthropist version: the specific, proportionate generosity that belongs in a life being built right now.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 54 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to think about how generosity fits into your life now, or when giving has been on your mind.

content.text: Five journal prompts for finding the version of generosity that belongs in your life at its current stage. Generosity that feels right at your level now is different from what you'll give when the wealth is larger. Both matter. Set twenty minutes and write without editing.
New to journalling as practice? Read
What does my current relationship with giving look like?
Not the aspirational version. The current one. When do you give easily? When do you hold back?
What does 'giving from fullness' feel like, compared to 'giving from fear of looking mean'?
Write the physical and emotional difference if there is one.
What generosity would feel right at my current level of wealth?
Specific acts, not abstract principles. The coffee for someone in the queue. The extra tip. The donation to a cause you care about. Name the ones that fit now.
What stops me from giving at that level already?
Write the actual reason, not the polished one.
If I made one small generous act a week, what would I choose?
Name it specifically, including the when and the how.
Adapted from Day 54 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
